description: Track all client sessions as named devices per user account. List, rename, and delete devices with cascading cleanup. Auto-purge devices inactive beyond retention period.

actors.name: Account Owner
actors.description: User managing their own registered devices

actors.name: Server Administrator
actors.description: Admin managing devices on behalf of users

actors.name: Homeserver
actors.description: Server enforcing device limits and running background purge tasks

fields.name: device_id
fields.label: Unique identifier for a client session on the user's account

fields.name: user_id
fields.label: User account the device belongs to

fields.name: display_name
fields.label: Human-readable label for the device; maximum 100 characters

validation.message: Device display name is too long (maximum 100 characters)

fields.name: created_ts
fields.label: Timestamp when the device was first registered

fields.name: last_seen_ts
fields.label: Timestamp of the most recent activity from this device

fields.name: last_seen_ip
fields.label: IP address of the device's most recent connection

values.description: Device is registered and has recent activity
Device has not been seen within the configured retention period
Device has been explicitly removed or purged

transitions.description: Device not accessed within the retention window
Background purge task removes the stale device

management: Each device is uniquely identified by the combination of user_id and device_id
Device display names must not exceed 100 characters
Deleting a device revokes its access tokens, removes its registered pushers, and cancels queued messages
Message cleanup after deletion is performed asynchronously
Devices inactive beyond the configured retention period are purged by a daily background task
Device list changes are broadcast to all users who share encrypted rooms with the account owner
A device cannot be deleted by another user unless the requester is a server administrator

given: user authenticates with a new or existing device_id
display_name does not exceed 100 characters if provided

then.description: Device record created or updated with display name and timestamps

device_registered.result: Device is listed in the user's device inventory

given: requester is the account owner or server administrator
new display name does not exceed 100 characters

then.description: Display name updated for the device

device_display_name_updated.result: Device shows the new label in device listings

given: requester is the account owner or server administrator

then.description: Device removed; associated tokens, pushers, and queued messages invalidated

device_deleted.result: Device can no longer be used; all associated sessions are terminated

given: device has not been accessed within the retention period

then.description: Stale device and all its associations removed

given: requester is authenticated as the account owner or server administrator
devices_listed.result: Full list of devices with metadata returned

errors.message: Device display name is too long (maximum 100 characters)
Device not found on this account

events.name: device.registered
events.description: A new device has been added to a user's account

events.name: device.updated
events.description: A device's display name was changed

events.name: device.deleted
events.description: A device was removed and its sessions invalidated

events.name: device.purged
events.description: A device was automatically removed by the stale-device cleanup task

events.name: device.list_retrieved
events.description: A user's device list was retrieved

related.reason: Cross-signing keys are associated with devices and updated on device changes
Device public keys are managed alongside device records
Pushers are removed when their associated device is deleted
